text: **Speaker 1:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**Speaker 1:** Jill.
**Speaker 1:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Speaker 1:** {Kids|子供たち} like to play {games|遊び/ゲーム}.
**Speaker 1:** But games are not just for {kids|子供たち}.
**Speaker 1:** Many people like to play games.
**Speaker 1:** And there are many {types|種類} of games, from {board games|ボードゲーム} and {computer games|コンピューターゲーム} to card games and {outdoor games|外で遊ぶゲーム}.
**Speaker 1:** What games do you play?
**Speaker 1:** Let's visit Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She helps us answer the question.
**Speaker 1:** What games do you play?
**Speaker 2:** Today, I want to play a game.
**Speaker 3:** Let's play {computer chess|コンピューターチェス}.
**Speaker 2:** No, Max, we played computer chess yesterday.
**Speaker 3:** You can play a {board game|ボードゲーム}.
**Speaker 2:** I like board games, but my board games are all {old|古い}.
**Speaker 2:** I want to play a new game.
**Speaker 2:** Max?
**Speaker 2:** Thanks.
**Speaker 2:** You can play an {outdoor game|外で遊ぶゲーム}.
**Speaker 2:** Daisy, it is hot today.
**Speaker 2:** I want to play {indoors|屋内で}.
**Speaker 3:** Ana, you play games, but do not win.
**Speaker 3:** Why do you play?
**Speaker 2:** Max, {winning|勝つこと} is not important.
**Speaker 2:** We play to have fun.
**Speaker 2:** You know, when I was a little girl, I was really good at {arcade games|アーケードゲーム}.
**Speaker 2:** I know what game to play today.
**Speaker 1:** Anna never wins at computer chess.
**Speaker 1:** She always loses.
**Speaker 1:** Her {board games|ボードゲーム} are old.
**Speaker 1:** She wants to play a {new|新しい} game.
**Speaker 1:** And she wants to play {indoors|屋内で|室内で}, not outdoors.
**Speaker 1:** Always and never are {opposites|反対/正反対}.
**Speaker 1:** Win and lose.
**Speaker 1:** New and old are {opposites|反対/正反対} too.
**Speaker 1:** Now listen to some sentences.
**Speaker 1:** Listen for opposites.
**Speaker 1:** Her hands are clean.
**Speaker 1:** His hands are {dirty|汚れた}.
**Speaker 1:** She is big.
**Speaker 1:** He is {small|小さい}.
**Speaker 1:** My grandmother is old.
**Speaker 1:** My little sister is {young|若い}.
**Speaker 1:** Now it is your turn.
**Speaker 1:** Try to find the opposite.
**Speaker 4:** Now you find the opposite.
**Speaker 4:** What is the opposite of light?
**Speaker 4:** Dark.
**Speaker 4:** What is the opposite of thick?
**Speaker 4:** Thin
**Speaker 4:** What is the opposite of low?
**Speaker 4:** Hi!
**Speaker 1:** Now let's hear from some friends.
**Speaker 1:** They answer the {question|質問}: What games do you play?
**Speaker 5:** I like to play {hide and seek|かくれんぼ} with my friends.
**Speaker 6:** I love playing {cards|トランプ}.
**Speaker 6:** I always win.
**Speaker 4:** I never win.
**Speaker 7:** I love to play {chess|チェス}.
**Speaker 7:** I {taught|教えた/指導した|/tɔːt/|I taught my sister math.} my mom how to play {chess|チェス/西洋将棋|/tʃes/|Chess is a strategy game.}
**Speaker 1:** Thanks friends!
**Speaker 1:** Those all sound like great {games|ゲーム/遊び|/geɪmz/|We play fun games together.}!
**Speaker 1:** Now let's go back to Anna.
**Speaker 1:** She wants to play a game from her {childhood|幼少期|/ˈtʃaɪldhʊd/|I have memories from my childhood.}
**Speaker 1:** What game is she going to play?
**Speaker 1:** Let's find out.
**Speaker 2:** They have {arcade|アーケード/ゲームセンター|/ˈɑːrkeɪd/|Arcade games are fun.} games!
**Speaker 2:** Let's go!
**Speaker 2:** When I was little, I played an arcade game called Maximum Overdrive
**Speaker 2:** I won all the {times|回数/時|/taɪmz/|Many times I tried.}
**Speaker 2:** I won!
**Speaker 2:** I won the {high score|最高得点|/haɪ skɔːr/|He got the high score.}!
**Speaker 2:** But my friends are not here.
**Speaker 2:** Maybe {winning|勝つこと|/ˈwɪnɪŋ/|Winning is not everything.} is not important.
**Speaker 3:** Did you win?
**Speaker 2:** Yeah, I won.
**Speaker 3:** Why are you {unhappy|不幸せ/不機嫌|/ʌnˈhæpi/|He looks unhappy today.}?
**Speaker 2:** Playing alone was not fun.
**Speaker 2:** So, do you want to play {computer|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtər/|Computer games are popular.} chess?
**Speaker 3:** Yes.
**Speaker 3:** Yes, I do.
**Speaker 1:** Now you can ask your friends, what do you want to play?
**Speaker 1:** And remember, winning is not always important.
**Speaker 1:** Playing games is fun, even when you lose.
**Speaker 1:** See you next time on Let's Learn English with Anna!
